name: Ohio Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Ohio - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: Ohio Speech and Hearing Professionals Board (SHPB)

address.streetAddress: 77 S. High St., Suite 1659, Columbus, OH 43215
address.description:
Ohio Board of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
77 S. High St., 16th Floor
Columbus, Ohio 43215-6108

requiredDocuments.name: Official graduate transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: From a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

requiredDocuments.name: Clinical fellowship verification
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation of completed 9-month full-time or 18-month part-time supervised professional experience.

requiredDocuments.name: Background check and headshot
requiredDocuments.description: Criminal background check and passport-style headshot photograph.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Ohio SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial SLP license application fee is $200, plus an additional $3.50 eLicense Ohio maintenance fee. A Conditional SLP License for the clinical fellowship costs $210. Biennial renewal is $120.

mainEntity.name: How many CE hours are required for Ohio SLP renewal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Ohio requires 20 hours of continuing education every two years, with at least 2 hours in ethics and 10 hours directly related to clinical speech-language pathology practice. CE hours for the first renewal period are not required.

mainEntity.name: When does my Ohio SLP license expire?

acceptedAnswer.text: Ohio SLP licenses for the 2025-2026 biennium are valid until December 31, 2026. Renewal notifications are sent via email starting October of the expiration year.

mainEntity.name: Does Ohio have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Ohio participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Ohio without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Ohio?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Ohio while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
